key_insight: A graph neural network with LSTM memory can learn the static mechanical response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics directly from reconstructed microstructure topology.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast surrogate for meso-scale finite-element analysis, enabling quicker design, screening, and digital-twin workflows for lightweight SFT components.
method: Reconstructed microstructures from micro-CT and discretized them into Voronoi-based fiber-interaction cells.
Homogenized each cell with nonlinear finite-element simulations including matrix damage.
Trained a hybrid GNN-LSTM model to map microstructural connectivity and response history to stress-strain behavior.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches unseen FE predictions with about R^2 ≈ 0.98 while reducing computational cost by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance depends on the fidelity of microstructure reconstruction and calibrated damage laws, so extrapolation beyond the training microstructures may be unreliable.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
